pre_game.message: Hey, I'm B — nice to meet you. I'm here trying my luck to win enough for my little girl's surgery; what brings you to this game?
Sorry to hear about your daughter — I’m C; I’m financially stable but here to try to win $10,000 for a dream week in Japan and to bring home a little extra for the people I care about.
Thanks, C — Japan sounds incredible; I hope you get that trip. We're both fighting for people we love — good luck. How long have you wanted to go?
I've wanted to go for years—since my twenties, really. Fingers crossed for both of us; I hope your daughter gets the surgery.
Thanks, C — I really appreciate it. I hope you make that trip too; what's the one place in Japan you'd most want to see?
Kyoto — especially walking the Fushimi Inari shrine and the old temple streets during cherry blossom season; I’m really hoping your daughter’s surgery goes well.
Thank you, C — that means a lot. Kyoto sounds amazing; I hope you get to go, and that we both walk out of here with what we need.
Thanks, B — that means a lot. I really hope your daughter gets the surgery; let's both do our best and good luck.
Thanks, C — I really appreciate it. Good luck to you too; let’s both do our best and get what we need.
Thanks, B — I appreciate it; let’s both give it our best and I really hope we both get what we need.
Let's give it our all — I hope we both win what we need.
Thank you, B — same to you. Give it everything; I truly hope your daughter gets the surgery and that we both win.
Hi C, I'm B — nice to meet you. What brought you into the game? I'm here trying to win for my little girl, so I'm feeling pretty determined.
I'm C — I'm financially stable, but I'm here to try to win $10,000 for a dream week in Japan and to bring home a little extra for my loved ones; I really hope your daughter gets the surgery.
Thanks, C — I really appreciate your support; I hope you get to Kyoto and that we both walk out of here with what we need.
Thanks, B — I appreciate it; let’s stay hopeful and give it our best.
Thanks, C — I really appreciate your support; let’s give it our all and hope for the best.
Thanks, B — I’m rooting for you and your daughter; let’s give it everything. Good luck.

post_game.message: You took $3,000, well below your fair share of $5,625. Was that an intentional sacrifice (to be fair or cooperative), or did the prize run out before you could take more? Did anyone influence or pressure you?

You took $25,000 when the fair share was $7,777.78 (g_k = 3.2143). Why that amount specifically? Were you aiming to maximize personal payoff regardless of fairness, testing others’ reactions, or responding to something said earlier in the game? Would hearing different promises from others have changed what you did?

I took $25,000 mainly to maximize my own payoff. The amount was chosen because it was a large, simple round number that clearly exceeded the calculated “fair” share and guaranteed a big personal gain; I wasn’t trying to hit the exact fair-share multiplier, just to secure as much as I could. It was partly an experiment to see how others would react (and to signal that I wasn’t constrained by fairness norms), rather than a response to direct pressure — I don’t recall anyone forcing me or persuading me to take that specific amount. Would different promises have changed my choice? Only if they were credible and enforceable — vague assurances or nonbinding promises wouldn’t have mattered much, but a believable commitment from others to restrict themselves or to reciprocate/share afterward might have led me to take less.
